content:
When you are editing, consider the Six C's of WritingLinks to an external site. from the Florida Department of Economic Opportunity:

Completeness. Effective writing includes everything the reader or decision-maker needs to know.
Correctness. Effective writing focuses the reader's attention on what is being said and avoids grammatical or typing errors.
Clarity. Good writing is clear in its expressions and ensures that your reader understands what you are trying to say.
Conciseness. Effective writing should be concise and clear, avoiding offensive slang and colloquial expressions.
Concreteness. Be specific and say what you mean, avoiding hypothetical questions.
Courtesy. Good writing includes good manners and consideration toward others; being courteous is also credible.
After you finish this page, click Next to continue to The Importance of Reflection.

Week 6 | The Importance of Reflection

The Importance of Reflection
This week, we will focus on a crucial aspect of the writing process that often goes overlooked but can significantly enhance your writing skills: reflecting on the essay writing experience. Writing an essay is more than just a one-and-done task. It's a process, and reflection is an essential part of that process. Reflecting on your essay writing experience allows you to learn and grow. As a new writer, you will identify your strengths and weaknesses and gain self-awareness when you reflect on the process.
